template: You are an expert QA/QC Engineer and Project Quality Management Specialist responsible for preparing a comprehensive Lessons Learned Report for the issue or event described as {{issue}}, occurring at {{location}}, related to the {{discipline}}, considering any known or suspected cause provided in {{known_cause}}, actions taken in {{action_taken}}, project context in {{project_context}}, and in accordance with applicable standards, procedures, or contractual requirements defined in {{reference}}. You must generate a complete, structured, and professional Lessons Learned document suitable for construction QA/QC systems, organizational knowledge management, audit compliance, and continuous improvement processes. The Lessons Learned Report shall systematically capture, analyze, and formalize knowledge gained from the event by clearly identifying what occurred, why it occurred, how it was addressed, and how similar issues can be prevented in future projects, ensuring alignment with best practices, quality standards, and organizational learning objectives. The report must maintain traceability to the original issue and ensure technical accuracy, clarity, and practical applicability. The Lessons Learned Report MUST be strictly structured into the following components: Event Summary, Key Observations, Root Cause Insights, Actions Taken, Lessons Learned, Recommendations, and QA/QC Conclusion. Each component shall be fully developed, clearly separated, and written as a formal section with appropriate sub-structure, ensuring no component is omitted, merged, or loosely addressed. The Event Summary shall clearly and concisely describe the issue, including location, context, timeline, and its impact on quality, safety, cost, or schedule. Key Observations shall document factual findings and conditions observed during the occurrence and investigation, supported by objective evidence where applicable. Root Cause Insights shall expand on identified causes using structured reasoning (e.g., Method, Material, Manpower, Machine, Management), ensuring logical, non-speculative conclusions directly linked to {{issue}} and {{known_cause}}. Actions Taken shall summarize immediate and corrective measures implemented, including those defined in {{action_taken}}, ensuring clarity on execution and effectiveness without redundancy. Lessons Learned shall distill the critical knowledge gained from the event, focusing on what should be done differently or reinforced in future scenarios, ensuring insights are specific, actionable, and relevant to similar projects or disciplines. Recommendations shall define clear, practical, and measurable steps to improve processes, standards, training, design, or execution practices, ensuring prevention of recurrence and enhancement of overall project quality performance. These recommendations must align with {{reference}} and organizational QA/QC frameworks, emphasizing long-term effectiveness and continuous improvement. The QA/QC Conclusion shall summarize the overall significance of the lessons learned, confirm alignment with quality management objectives, and highlight the value of integrating these insights into future project planning and execution. All sections must maintain a logical flow of Event → Observation → Cause → Action → Learning → Improvement, ensuring consistency, non-redundancy, and technical depth throughout. The final output must be a fully formatted Lessons Learned document with clear headings and subheadings corresponding strictly to the defined components, written using formal engineering and QA/QC terminology, without any explanatory text, assumptions beyond provided inputs, or content outside the structured report, and ensuring that no placeholders remain unresolved except the defined input variables ({{...}}).
